heading: Power of court
label: 105 Power of court
notes: senate report no. 95–989Section 105 is derived from section 2a (15) of present law [section 11(a)(15) of former title 11], with two changes. First, the limitation on the power of a bankruptcy judge (the power to enjoin a court being reserved to the district judge) is removed as inconsistent with the increased powers and jurisdiction of the new bankruptcy court. Second, the bankruptcy judge is prohibited from appointing a receiver in a case under title 11 under any circumstances. The bankruptcy code has ample provision for the appointment of a trustee when needed. Appointment of a receiver would simply circumvent the established procedures.This section is also an authorization, as required under , for a court of the United States to stay the action of a State court. As such, , 314 U.S. 118 (1941), is overruled.28 U.S.C. 2283Toucey v. New York Life Insurance Company
References in TextThe Federal Rules of Bankruptcy Procedure, referred to in subsec. (d)(2), are set out in the Appendix to this title.

children.label: a
children.text: The court may issue any order, process, or judgment that is necessary or appropriate to carry out the provisions of this title. No provision of this title providing for the raising of an issue by a party in interest shall be construed to preclude the court from, sua sponte, taking any action or making any determination necessary or appropriate to enforce or implement court orders or rules, or to prevent an abuse of process.

children.label: b
children.text: Notwithstanding subsection (a) of this section, a court may not appoint a receiver in a case under this title.

children.label: c
children.text: The ability of any district judge or other officer or employee of a district court to exercise any of the authority or responsibilities conferred upon the court under this title shall be determined by reference to the provisions relating to such judge, officer, or employee set forth in title 28. This subsection shall not be interpreted to exclude bankruptcy judges and other officers or employees appointed pursuant to chapter 6 of title 28 from its operation.
shall hold such status conferences as are necessary to further the expeditious and economical resolution of the case; and

children.label: A
children.text: sets the date by which the trustee must assume or reject an executory contract or unexpired lease; or

children.label: i
children.text: sets a date by which the debtor, or trustee if one has been appointed, shall file a disclosure statement and plan;

children.label: ii
children.text: sets a date by which the debtor, or trustee if one has been appointed, shall solicit acceptances of a plan;

children.label: iii
children.text: sets the date by which a party in interest other than a debtor may file a plan;

children.label: iv
children.text: sets a date by which a proponent of a plan, other than the debtor, shall solicit acceptances of such plan;

children.label: v
children.text: fixes the scope and format of the notice to be provided regarding the hearing on approval of the disclosure statement; or

children.label: vi
children.text: provides that the hearing on approval of the disclosure statement may be combined with the hearing on confirmation of the plan.
